refactor: migrate 4189 hardcoded color pairs to semantic design tokens

Define 15 semantic color tokens (surface, muted, subtle, body, strong,
heading, edge, link, accent, success, warning, danger) in CSS @theme
with light/dark intermediary variables. Each token auto-switches between
modes — no dark: prefix needed.

Codemod replaces contiguous light+dark class pairs across 114 component
files: bg-white dark:bg-slate-800 → bg-surface, text-slate-500
dark:text-slate-400 → text-muted, border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-700
→ border-edge, etc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): drop native-tls to fix Linux ARM64 cross-compile

The aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu build failed because openssl-sys could
not find cross-compiled OpenSSL headers. The root cause was
azure_core defaulting to reqwest_native_tls, which pulls in
openssl-sys even though reqwest is already configured with rustls-tls.

Disable default features on azure_core and azure_identity, keeping
only reqwest + deflate + gzip + tokio. Since reqwest already has
rustls-tls from our direct dependency, TLS works without any system
OpenSSL installation — eliminating the cross-compile failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add missing tests for resumeAt=2 path in regex expression conditional (#126657)

## Description

The Code Review workflow on PR #126561 identified that the new tests
cover `resumeAt=0` (yes-branch) and `resumeAt=1` (no-branch) paths but
miss the `resumeAt=2` pass-through path — expression conditional with
only a yes-branch (no no-branch) inside a loop.

Adds 3 test cases to `Regex.MultipleMatches.Tests.cs`:

- **Yes-only conditional, condition always fails** —
`(?((?'-1'))(?'1'.)+)+(?!(?'-1'))` with `"abc"` exercises the
pass-through path producing empty matches at each position (guarded with
`#if !NETFRAMEWORK` since .NET Framework produces 3 empty matches vs 4
on .NET Core due to different empty-match-at-end-of-string semantics)
- **Yes-only conditional with prefix capture, even input** —
`((?'1'.)(?((?'-1'))(?'1'.)))+` with `"abcd"` exercises the
yes-branch-taken path inside a loop
- **Yes-only conditional with prefix capture, odd input** — same pattern
with `"abc"` verifying partial match behavior

The latter two test cases are the primary coverage for the `|| isInLoop`
fix, since their non-backtracking yes-branch means `isInLoop` is the
necessary trigger for `resumeAt = 2` (the old condition
`postYesDoneLabel != originalDoneLabel` would have been false). These
run on both .NET Core and .NET Framework.

All 32,100 existing tests continue to pass.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: danmoseley <6385855+danmoseley@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Dan Moseley <danmose@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(sensor): graceful state — 0 for car_off, last-known during API outage

Three related improvements to prevent HA cards from disappearing or
showing error banners when data is temporarily absent:

sensor.py:
- available always returns True; cards never blank out
- native_value returns 0 when sync_status is car_off
- native_value returns last-known value during api_offline for up to
  SENSOR_API_OFFLINE_GRACE_S (60 s), then falls back to 0
- _handle_health_update unconditionally writes state (native_value
  depends on sync_status, not just availability)
- Track _last_live_at (monotonic) on every SSE reading

const.py:
- Add SENSOR_API_OFFLINE_GRACE_S = 60

coordinator.py:
- _was_ever_live flag: car-off debounce only starts after at least one
  confirmed live reading, preventing false 'live' window at startup
- Clear _car_off_since_mono on API poll failure so stale elapsed time
  does not count toward the debounce threshold across outages

tests: add 11 new tests covering value fallback, grace window,
_was_ever_live gating, and timer-clear on API failure; update 4 tests
that checked available=False (now available=True with value=0)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: proper td nodes in data model, remove tr auto-wrap hack

Rows have explicit {tag:td, text:...} children. Renderer is generic
again — no special tr→td wrapping. Add Speaker creates td nodes and
copies+splits into td/text paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add missing `return` after `sendError(403)` in eform PDF generation servlets (#1352)

* fix: add missing return after sendError(403) in PDF generation servlets

Add `return;` after `response.sendError(HttpServletResponse.SC_FORBIDDEN)` in
EFormSignatureViewForPdfGenerationServlet and EFormImageViewForPdfGenerationServlet.

Without the return, execution continued after the 403 response was committed,
potentially throwing IllegalStateException or NullPointerException when subsequent
code accessed request parameters or called RequestDispatcher.forward().

These two servlets now match the correct pattern already used in
EFormViewForPdfGenerationServlet and EformViewForPdfGenerationServlet.

Fixes CWE-698 (Execution After Redirect).

Fixes #<issue>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: restrict q quit to root views only

q now goes back (same as Esc) when in Build History or Log Viewer,
and only quits from the three root views (Dashboard, Pipelines,
Active Runs). Ctrl+C still force-quits from anywhere.

This prevents accidental exits when navigated deep into a pipeline.

test(integration): validate Ruby 3.0.2 and Puma 5 support (#1767)

* test(integration): validate Ruby 3.0.2 and Puma 5 support

Add a dedicated Rails integration target that runs the historical Ruby 3.0.2 and Puma 5.x stack instead of inferring support from repository history alone.

This change adds:

- a Ruby 3.0.2 / Puma 5.6.6 test image with a locked compatibility Gemfile

- a dedicated docker-compose suite for that stack

- runtime assertions that confirm the Ruby and Puma versions exercised by the Rails suites

- a small compose exec helper used by the version checks

This keeps the work scoped to validation for the Ruby support issue and avoids writing support-matrix documentation in this branch.

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Set bundle path in img

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): replace black with ruff format to resolve formatter conflict (#99)

The pre-commit config uses ruff-format as the canonical formatter, but
ci-quality-gate.yml and ci-standard.yml ran black --check which
produces different output, causing CI failures. Align all workflows
and the Makefile to use ruff format consistently.

commit.message:
fix(branches): scope members tab authorization to specific branch

The Members tab on Branch view had two security issues:
- Members were eagerly loaded via contain() regardless of permissions
- Authorization check used global table-level canIndex instead of
  branch-scoped check

Now creates a probe Member entity with the branch ID and checks
canIndex against it, so BasePolicy._hasPolicy properly validates
the user permission for that specific branch. Members data is
only loaded when authorized.

Applies to both view() and edit() actions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: web GUI only reads config, never patches server on init

Remove createDefaultConfig() and all patch_global_json calls from
the GUI startup flow. loadConfig() is now purely read-only:
- Fetches /cpp_cli_srv_config via get_global_json
- If found: applies it to disable/enable Run buttons
- If missing or unreachable: commandConfig={} (all buttons enabled)

Config must be set manually on the server side if needed.

commit.message:
refactor: extract LogViewerState sub-struct from App

Extract 14 log viewer fields into a dedicated LogViewerState struct
with Default impl. This replaces manual field-by-field resets in
go_back() and navigate_to_log_viewer() with clean struct resets.

- go_back() now resets via `self.log_viewer = LogViewerState::default()`
- navigate_to_log_viewer() uses struct literal with `..Default::default()`
- All callsites updated: app.field -> app.log_viewer.field for log viewer
  state across main.rs, events.rs, ui/logs.rs, app/timeline.rs

App struct reduced from 36 to 22 public fields. (a1, a3, a5)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
sec: Validate email params in EmailAttachmentSettings.of() before session storage (#1489)

* sec: validate email params in EmailAttachmentSettings.of() before session storage

Adds input validation and sanitization for 7 raw user input fields in the
EmailAttachmentSettings.of() factory method:
- senderEmail: validate email format regex
- subjectEmail: strip CR/LF (prevents SMTP header injection), max 200 chars
- bodyEmail: max 10000 chars
- emailPDFPassword: strip control chars, max 100 chars
- emailPDFPasswordClue: strip control chars, max 100 chars
- encryptedMessageEmail: max 10000 chars
- emailPatientChartOption: validate against known values (doNotAddAsNote, addFullNote)

Fixes output encoding in emailCompose.jsp:
- emailPatientChartOption hidden field: use e:forHtmlAttribute()
- emailPDFPassword input: use e:forHtmlAttribute()

Adds nosemgrep comments for false positive session read/cleanup lines in
AddEForm2Action (lines 163, 165).

Adds unit tests for all validation methods.

Fixes carlos-emr/carlos#1451


Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: yingbull <8680161+yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Michael Yingbull <michael@maplecreekmedical.ca>

commit.message:
feat(eval): add tool_definitions for Foundry tool evaluators

Include QPrisma's 17 LangGraph agent tool schemas (OpenAI function-calling
format) in the evaluation data file so that Foundry tool evaluators can
assess tool selection and usage accuracy.

Changes:
- Add tool_definitions.py with all 17 tool schemas (InjectedState params excluded)
- Update generate_eval_data.py to embed tool_definitions in each data item
- Add data_mapping to route tool_definitions to evaluator inputs
- Add builtin.tool_selection and builtin.tool_output_utilization evaluators

Tool evaluators that now receive tool_definitions:
- tool_call_accuracy (was failing: 'Tool definitions input is required')
- tool_selection (new)
- tool_output_utilization (new)
- tool_call_success (doesn't need tool_definitions, but benefits from context)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: parallel fan-out/fan-in + labeled predecessor outputs

Problems fixed:
1. resolveNextNode only returned first edge target — fan-out to parallel
   branches (e.g. 毛泽东+马斯克) was silently dropped
2. Fan-in (join) missing — 巴菲特 ran without waiting for both predecessors
3. availableInputs flat-merged all outputs (key collisions, lost source info)
4. ctx.messages truncated to last 3 — downstream nodes got incomplete history

Fixes:
- Replace resolveNextNode with resolveNextNodes returning all valid targets
- Add getNodePredecessors + isNodeReady helpers for join detection
- Add pendingParallelNodes: string[] to ExecutionContext
- completeNode: fan-out → queue extra targets; fan-in → check all preds done
  before advancing; pop pendingParallelNodes when join not yet ready
- availableInputs now { globalContext, predecessorOutputs: { nodeLabel: output } }
  so each node clearly sees which output came from which predecessor
- Remove messages.slice(-3) — pass full message history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

for effective soft-wrapping. Update .markdownlint.yml line_length to
match. Add .prettierignore to exclude test fixtures. Update README
template and fix-readme hook for prettier compatibility. Accept all
prettier-reformatted markdown files.

sec: Fix 7 genuine S5131 XSS vulnerabilities across 5 JSP files (#1404)

* sec: fix 7 genuine S5131 XSS vulnerabilities across 5 JSP files

Fixes #1403

- billingCodeSearch.jsp: encode nameF with Encode.forJavaScript() in script context
- billingCodeUpdate.jsp: encode nameF with Encode.forJavaScript() in script context
- billingReport_unbilled.jspf: wrap URLEncoder.encode() with Encode.forJavaScriptAttribute()
  and fix deprecated single-arg URLEncoder.encode() to use UTF-8 charset
- demo_select.jsp: URL-encode orderby parameter with URLEncoder.encode(orderby, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
- providerencountersingle.jsp: encode content with Encode.forHtml() in XML data island

  token refresh, handle_action, data refresh, and error messages (c2, c3)
- Add info tracing for view navigation (navigate_to_log_viewer)
- Derive Debug on Action enum to support tracing format
- Replace 5-element TaskEntry tuple with named TaskCandidate struct
  in timeline.rs for readability (a6)

commit.message:
fix: FM Tracker (.fmt) always uses native parser — UADE can't play PC OPL formats

Removed the preference gate for fmTracker format and default to native.
UADE is an Amiga replayer and cannot handle PC OPL-based FM Tracker files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): indent PROMPT continuation lines in release workflow

Lines 117-127 of the multi-line PROMPT shell variable had zero
indentation, which broke out of the YAML block scalar (run: |).
GitHub parsed them as top-level YAML instead of shell script content,
causing an "invalid workflow file" syntax error on line 117.

Indent the continuation lines to match the block indentation level
so they stay inside the scalar. The YAML parser strips the leading
spaces, so the prompt content sent to the LLM is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add Chi Siamo link to SPA footer for E-E-A-T discovery

Add About Us link before Contact in footer so crawlers (squirrelscan,
Googlebot) can discover the /chi-siamo page from the SPA-rendered app.
Previously the about page was only reachable via direct URL or static
HTML nav — the React footer had Contact, Privacy, Terms but no About.

Added footer.aboutUs i18n key in all 4 locales + critical bundles.

commit.message:
Add CI update channel with auto-publish on push to master

- Add publish job to build.yml that deploys Velopack packages to the
  GitHub Pages feed when triggered by a push (not PRs). CI packages
  coexist with nightly in the same updates/ directory via separate
  channel-scoped release manifests (releases.{rid}-ci.json).
- Add UpdateChannel.CI enum value.
- Recognize -ci as a valid update channel in UpdateService so
  CI-installed builds auto-update. CI uses the same GitHub Pages feed
  URL as nightly; the channel suffix differentiates the manifests.
- CI channel is auto-detected only — not exposed in the Settings UI
  ComboBox.
- Rename NightlyFeedUrl to GitHubPagesFeedUrl since it is now shared
  by both nightly and CI channels.
- Update doc comments and UI notice text to reflect CI as a recognized
  channel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
